title: Conjecture 1.1 (Hadwiger's Conjecture)
statement_text: Let $G$ be a graph. If $\chi(G)\geqslant t$, then $G$ has $K_{t}$ as a minor. Equivalently, if $G$ does not have $K_{t}$ as a minor, then $\chi(G)<t$.
context_text: Hadwiger's conjecture, formulated in 1943, is one of the central open problems in graph theory. It is only known to hold for $t\leqslant 6$, and is stated here as the motivation and starting point for the odd Hadwiger conjecture studied in the paper.
paper_contribution: Not directly addressed; the paper disproves the odd Hadwiger conjecture (a strict strengthening of Hadwiger's conjecture), but Hadwiger's conjecture itself remains open.
paper_title: Disproof of the Odd Hadwiger Conjecture

title: Conjecture 1.2 (Odd Hadwiger Conjecture)
statement_text: Let $G$ be a graph. If $\chi(G)\geqslant t$, then $G$ has $K_{t}$ as an odd minor. Equivalently, if $G$ does not have $K_{t}$ as an odd minor, then $\chi(G)<t$.
context_text: Proposed by Gerards and Seymour in 1993, the odd Hadwiger conjecture strengthens Hadwiger's conjecture by replacing the ordinary minor relation with the parity-sensitive notion of an odd minor. The conjecture was known for $t\leqslant 5$ but remained wide open for $t\geqslant 6$.
paper_contribution: Disproves the conjecture in a strong form by constructing graphs that contain no $K_t$ as an odd minor yet have chromatic number at least $\left(\tfrac{3}{2}-o(1)\right)t$.
paper_title: Disproof of the Odd Hadwiger Conjecture
